result:
This confirms it â€” this is the wpa_supplicant/hostapd `dl_list` implementation, and it's **header-only**. There is no `src/utils/list.c` in this repo (confirmed via `find`: only `list.h` exists).

## `struct dl_list` definition
`src/utils/list.h:15-18`
```c
struct dl_list {
	struct dl_list *next;
	struct dl_list *prev;
};
```
A minimal intrusive doubly-linked list node (circular, sentinel-style â€” an empty list has `next == prev == self`).

## Operating functions/macros â€” all in `list.h` (no `.c` file exists)

Confirmed via `documentSymbol` on the header:

| Name | Line | Kind | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `dl_list_init` | 22 | `static inline` function | Initialize list head as empty (self-pointing) |
| `dl_list_add` | 28 | `static inline` function | Insert `item` right after `list` (head-insert) |
| `dl_list_add_tail` | 36 | `static inline` function | Insert `item` at the end (calls `dl_list_add(list->prev, item)`) |
| `dl_list_del` | 41 | `static inline` function | Unlink `item` from its list, then NULL its `next`/`prev` |
| `dl_list_empty` | 49 | `static inline` function | Check if list is empty (`list->next == list`) |
| `dl_list_len` | 54 | `static inline` function | Count entries by walking `next` pointers |
| `dl_list_entry` | 67 | macro | Container-of: recover enclosing struct pointer from a `dl_list*` member |
| `dl_list_first` | 70 | macro | Get first entry (or `NULL` if empty), via `dl_list_entry` |
| `dl_list_last` | 74 | macro | Get last entry (or `NULL` if empty) |
| `dl_list_for_each` | 78 | macro | Forward iteration loop |
| `dl_list_for_each_safe` | 83 | macro | Forward iteration, safe against removal of current item during traversal |
| `dl_list_for_each_reverse` | 89 | macro | Backward iteration loop |
| `DEFINE_DL_LIST` | 94 | macro | Declare + statically initialize a new empty list variable |
| `DL_LIST_HEAD_INIT` | 20 | macro | Static initializer expression `{ &l, &l }` |

Note: `dl_list.h:45-46` triggers a clangd diagnostic (`undeclared identifier 'NULL'`) because this header, taken in isolation, doesn't `#include <stddef.h>` â€” it relies on including translation units to bring in `NULL` first. Not something to fix per your instructions, just flagging it since LSP surfaced it.
